candidates.stmt:
Balsall & Berkswell is my home, it's where I have built my life and raised my children.
This community is special, full of wonderful, kind people who are there for each other.
I believe in working for residents- for their priorities, focusing on working to make a difference on the things that matter to those who live here- green spaces, our roads and infrastructure, protecting our greenbelt wherever possible.
I am here all year round- for you.

I am standing for re-election as a councillor in Balsall and Berkswell. I have had the privilege of representing the area where I live for nearly 14 years. The reasons why I originally stood for election are still valid: I want to protect our local area from inappropriate development and also from the harmful effects of HS2 construction. The best way of keeping Solihull as a great place to live, is to vote Conservative on 7th May.

I would like to introduce myself as the Labour candidate for Bickenhill.

I am a retired librarian and I have lived in Solihull for over 40 years. I am very active in the local community, including being a volunteer for Citizens Advice and a support assistant in adult education.

I believe strongly in giving something back to the community and that is why I am standing to be a Labour Councillor. I would like to make a real difference to people’s lives in Bickenhill and I would be a strong voice in the Council, fighting to get the best services for the residents.

Bickenhill is a diverse area, with a range of different problems. In particular, I would fight for an increase in affordable housing, so that young people especially could get on the housing ladder. I would also campaign for increased funding for youth services which have been decimated in the austerity years. Partly because of this, there has been a big increase in anti-social behaviour such as motor bike racing and fly tipping, which cause problems for local people.

The pandemic has made many of us consider what kind of life we want for ourselves and our community. What is the point of returning to the status quo, when the pandemic has highlighted the inequality endemic in our society? We need a more caring society with vibrant and well-funded public services to tackle fundamental problems and that is what I pledge to fight for.

I have lived in Solihull’s Blythe ward for 30 years and after becoming increasingly concerned and frustrated at both National and local government policies and wasteful spending of hard earned tax payers money I have decided that to change things where it is possible. Residents deserve services they can rely on and efficient use of resources.
Listening to local residents concerns and issues like planning, pot holes and ASB that affect our communities. I will bring a common sense approach that is needed to change the current process.

Jamie Walker: Veteran. Resident. Father.
As a decorated Army veteran and a Blyth resident, I believe leadership is about service, not politics. I’m standing as your Reform UK candidate for Solihull because our community deserves straight talk, real-world experience, and most importantly action.
For too long, the mainstream parties have ignored local people while wasting money on vanity projects. As a father, I want my children to grow up in a community that works. I am bringing the discipline and accountability of the Armed Forces to the council to ensure every pound of your money is spent wisely, transparently, and effectively.
My Priorities for Solihull:
- Put Local People First: Restoring common sense to decision-making and ensuring your voice is actually heard.
- Youth & Community Collaboration: Integrating our youth with community-giving initiatives. By giving young people meaningful ways to contribute, we build mutual respect and a more united ward.
- Safety & Security: Standing up for a safer, more accountable community where residents feel protected.
- Protect Our Heritage: Defending our green belt and supporting our rural farming communities from over-development.
- True Accountability: Bringing a veteran’s sense of duty to local government, no more excuses, just results.

Solihull deserves leadership that listens and acts. If you want a representative who isn't afraid to speak the truth and lead with harmony, I would be proud to have your support.

It’s time for a change. It’s time for Reform.

#ReformUK #Solihull #Blyth #CommonSense

I’m Dermot Reid, standing for Reform UK in Blythe.

I live in Tidbury Green with my partner and young son, so like many of you, I care about the basics being right, safe communities, well-run services, and council tax being spent properly.

I spent nearly 14 years in the Royal Navy Submarine Service, working in a close-knit crew where there’s no room for passengers and nowhere to hide from responsibility. You learn quickly to make decisions, take ownership, and get things done properly. That’s a big part of why I’ve decided to stand.

In my day job, I manage large contracts and budgets across buildings and services. It’s about accountability, planning, and making sure things are delivered properly and not just talked about. That’s exactly the approach I’d bring to how the council runs services and spends your money.

Too often, decisions are made without real accountability and residents are left dealing with the consequences. You can see that in places like Blythe Valley, where many feel underrepresented or Tidbury Green where every blade of grass seems to have a planning proposal pending.

And importantly good leadership isn’t just about making decisions, it’s about being willing to review them and change course when they’re no longer working (cough cough, cycle lanes).

If elected, my focus will be simple:
* Clear accountability on spending
* Better oversight so services don’t slip
* A practical, common-sense approach, including revisiting decisions that aren’t working
* Fairer representation for all our communities
* Regularly feeding back to residents so people know what’s being done and why
* Protecting the character of our area while managing growth properly
* Supporting local businesses and keeping our communities thriving

I’m not a career politician, just someone who deals with real responsibility every day and wants to bring that same approach to the council.

On May 7th, you have 3 votes. I’d really appreciate your support.

Dermot Reid
Reform UK Candida

Low Council Tax and quality front-line Council services are my priorities. I am a Chartered Accountant who has lived with my wife in Solihull Borough for 27 years. I will work with our Conservative Councillors and the local police to bear down on antisocial behaviour, burglary and drug related crime. We will continue to work to reduce inconsiderate parking and speeding. Castle Bromwich still retains parks and green spaces. If elected, I will cherish these and work with community groups to improve the amenity, whilst protecting them from developers. I will promote local businesses and jobs. The improved transport links promised by Conservative Mayor Andy Street are welcome. I will be alert to the risk of additional through traffic or parking problems brought about by the changes. I will encourage schemes for home insulation as well as "levelling up" our community housing. Solihull Council is Conservative-led. It is important to elect Conservative Councillors so that we have strong voices raising our local issues in the Council.

As your dedicated Green councillor since 2021, I have passionately served our community by addressing critical local issues such as social housing, potholes, and safer streets. My commitment to being always accessible to residents has allowed me to stand with you and advocate for your concerns in the council, ensuring that your voices are heard and acted upon. Living in our constituency with my family for over 12 years has given me a deep understanding of our needs and challenges. This firsthand experience, combined with my environmentalist background, drives my commitment to creating a sustainable and thriving community. I believe in practical solutions that benefit everyone, from improving our local infrastructure to working closely with the police to enhance safety. One of the most urgent issues we face is the need for decisive climate action by the next government. As your MP, I will ensure that the government is held accountable for its promises and takes meaningful steps towards addressing climate change. I am dedicated to fostering a fair, green, and prosperous future for all residents. I am proud of the support and appreciation I have received from residents across all backgrounds and age groups, and I am eager to continue serving you as your MP. Together, we can create a better, brighter future for our constituency. Let's work together for a greener, fairer, and safer community.

I grew up in Knowle, attending primary school in Dorridge. After been a lifelong local volunteer for Girl Guides, Age UK Solihull and as a governor for Solihull College I became a councillor in 2024. 
I have found supporting residents with local issues including planning, highways, green spaces and local issue hugely rewarding. Caring about residents concerns and supporting Solihull to remain the fantastic place it is to live and work is the most rewarding thing I have ever done. 
I hope residents continue to put their faith in me in 2026.

David Brittin has lived in Solihull for more than 40 years and he and his wife Margaret have for the past 20 years made their home in Dorridge, where he is an active member of the local community. After a career in newspaper journalism and public relations, he re-trained as a teacher of literacy. He is a history graduate of the University of Warwick.

If elected he pledges:

To back more affordable housing for first-time buyers who are often forced by a shortage of this kind of property to move from the area where they have grown up. To defend the green belt which helps make Solihull such an attractive place but is under constant threat. To campaign to make Solihull Council a Real Living Wage employer in line with the majority of local authorities in the West Midlands.

I’m Georgina Hartland, one of your Conservative candidates for Elmdon.

I’ve been out across the ward with the Elmdon Action Team, reporting issues like fly-tipping and potholes and helping to improve our streets and neighbourhoods.

Together, we’ve carried out a crime survey to better understand residents’ concerns and how safe people feel locally.

If elected, I’ll continue working hard to tackle the issues that matter to you in Elmdon.

I’ve been fortunate enough to have been a Solihull resident for over 25 years. During that time I have seen the changes in the town, some of which are very positive but others have seen us move backwards. We have had a local council which has been driving in one direction far too long. The time really has come for us to have a change. It’s needed quite badly. We have seen the delivery of local services suffer, more empty shops in the town centre, increasing traffic problems and only a half-hearted attempt to take local people’s views into account. All of these need correcting and can be tackled by greater balance on the council, which in turn can be delivered by Labour.

Just as a resurgent Labour party in Westminster has forced multiple essential U-turns from the Conservative central government, where the Conservative policy has been both wrong and out of step with the views of the country, so the same can be done locally with a stronger Labour voice.

My name is Adrian, and I am standing to be your voice on Solihull Council for Kingshurst & Smith’s Wood.
​I’m not a career politician. I’m a local dad with a young family, working hard every day to provide for them. I see the same issues you do: the state of our roads, the need for safer streets, and the importance of looking after our community.
​In my job, I solve problems and make sure things run properly. I want to bring that same practical, 'no-nonsense' approach to the Council. I believe we need more people in the Town Hall who understand the value of hard work and honest service.
​If you want someone who will listen, work hard, and put our local area first, I would be honored to have your vote this Thursday.

I am a local resident of Kingshurst and Smith’s Wood standing as a candidate for local councillor. I am actively involved in the community, working on issues such as tackling crime and anti-social behaviour, reducing speeding, and improving local facilities.

I regularly engage with residents through surveys and petitions, report local issues, and campaign for action on fly-tipping, litter, and road repairs. I am focused on providing visible, responsive and proactive representation for the area year-round

I have been proud to serve Knowle Ward as a Councillor for five years now and to have made things happen for individuals, groups and the community as a whole in that time. Whether dealing with major issues, such as inappropriate developments, or flooding, or all too frequent irritants, eg potholes and overgrown hedges, I have been pleased to intervene and get results.
I am also very active as a Trustee Director of Knowle Society and Founder Director of Visit Knowle.
I am particularly proud to have been or currently being at the heart of the demands to rejuvenate the Precinct, the need for a new life for the former Station Road toilet block, the new planters in Swan Yard, the annual Christmas Lights Switch-on, the Jubilee, Coronation and VE80 celebrations and those for the forthcoming 750th Anniversary of Knowle
A resident since the early eighties, I remain eager to keep Knowle a great place to live. I like to think that through all of these activities, I make a significant contribution to our community life and provide a strong and well connected representative voice for our residents and businesses.

Yvonne Clements has experience as a Councillor and is part of a strong local team with Cllr David Pinwell and Cllr Edward Fitter. She has lived in the Arden Triangle for many years and had connections with Knowle for 50 years.
Her passion and raison d’etre is community and the fabric of this is education, healthcare, crime and looking after our streets and support our High Street.
She will hold a Councillor surgery if elected, on a monthly basis, to allow residents to discuss any concerns or issues.

Edward is a local candidate, he grew up in Knowle and Dorridge and attended Arden School. He is active in Knowle village life and volunteers at local community events. He captained Knowle U18 FC and played for Chadwick End FC. Edward has been a councillor for Knowle since 2023 and he is known for his energy and hands-on approach. He works hard to fix local issues and is a highly visible councillor. Working with the community, he has been successful at protecting Knowle against unsustainable developments, and he champions protecting our green spaces. He is standing for re-election on Thursday 7th May for Knowle, Barston and Chadwick End.

Edward is a part of a strong team for Knowle along with Dave Pinwell and Yvonne Clements.

I’m Ade Adeyemo, your Liberal Democrat candidate for Solihull West & Shirley.

With proven leadership within Solihull Council and deep roots in our community, I am the only candidate from Solihull with a genuine chance of winning, the only one who can truly be trusted to represent your interests.

As a proud Solihull resident for over 27 years, I am trusted and respected across the borough as a local councillor. I understand the unique challenges and opportunities that our community faces.

I have consistently worked to improve our local services, support our businesses, and enhance the quality of life for all residents. As your MP, I will continue to prioritise these goals and work tirelessly to make Solihull a better place for everyone.

I believe in a Solihull where our schools are better funded, our healthcare services work well and our local businesses thrive. We can build a future where our children have more opportunities, our streets are safer, and our environment is protected.

My three main goals are as follows:  •  Health and Social Care  •  The Cost of Living Crisis  •  More Police and PCSOs on our Streets

A vote for Labour in Solihull is a wasted vote. Labour has never had an MP in Solihull and never will. You must vote Liberal Democrat if you don't want another disappointing Conservative MP. Lib Dems have won here before, and we can do so again.

What I am offering is real change in Solihull - an opportunity for a hopeful and positive future. On July 4th, vote Ade Adeyemo and help us break the routine. Let's turn the page and begin a positive chapter with hope for a better future for Solihull.

Your vote can make a real difference. Vote for change. Vote Ade Adeyemo.

Gerard is a local resident, having lived in the Shirley East ward for 30 years with his wife who was born in Solihull. He has two children who, although now grown up, benefited from the excellent schooling afforded in the locality. Working in financial services for 38 years has helped Gerard to appreciate the value of a secure home and standard of living, and as a parent, the benefits of a good education and access to good health care. If elected he will be campaigning for these basic fundamental rights for all residents.

GERARD’S PLEDGES:

WORK FOR ALL LOCAL RESIDENTS – irrespective of where they live or who they vote for. BETTER GP SERVICES – everyone deserves equal access to decent medical care through their GPs. I will make this a priority. FUNDING FOR LOCAL SCHOOLS – our children should be educated to the highest standard irrespective of wealth or status. AFFORDABLE HOUSING – we need a much better balance of housing to provide for young families as well as retirement homes.

I’m standing in this year’s local elections because I want Shirley South to have a Labour councillor who listens, responds, and gets things done. After 29 years living in Solihull, I know the strengths of our community and the pressures many residents are dealing with. My focus is to ensure people here feel supported, respected, and properly represented.

If elected, I will prioritise the issues residents raise most often: safer streets, cleaner neighbourhoods, and better access to essential services. Many people have told me they’re frustrated by slow action on problems like fly‑tipping, potholes, and anti‑social behaviour. I want to work closely with residents, local groups, and the council to make sure these concerns are addressed promptly and effectively. My aim is straightforward: to help make our area a better place to live for everyone.

*Vote Stuart Hedley for Shirley South on 7th May*

I am a fierce defender of justice and peace. I am a teacher, for the last 22years I have dedicated myself to social change through education. It is my honour to engage pupils who have previously struggled to engage in the school system. My Buddhist practice enables me to stay grounded and focused whilst making waves in society. I work together with other faiths and those without faith to run events in Solihull. Our next big event will take place on 8th September at Warwick University. Watch this space! I am aligned to Green Party policies of social and environmental justice and inclusion. I am completing a postgraduate Law degree and enjoy playing the violin and singing- once in a while I spend time with my husband, my 18 year old houseguest and my two cats. Laboremus.

Chris was born in Solihull and has lived here for most of his life. As a chartered accountant who has made a career of working closely with businesses of all sizes, he understands the needs and concerns of the business community. He is committed to working hard to maintain and build upon the mix of nature and urban living that makes Shirley an incredible place to live, work and raise a family.

Josh was born and raised in Solihull and has lived here his entire life. With a strong track record in local public service, (previous experience in local government as a Councillor), he knows how to gets things done—tackling everyday issues, listening to residents, and delivering practical results for the community and can hit the ground running if elected in Silhill Ward on May 7th.

He’s hands-on, active in local community groups, and not afraid to roll up his sleeves to make a difference. Working with the existing Conservative-led administration, Josh champions residents’ voices and fights to protect what makes Solihull successful and special.

Hitesh Bikumalla has proudly called Silhill home for over 12 years. He has built strong connections within the community, working closely with local groups and supporting residents.

Hitesh and his wife run a small business in Solihull, and their daughter attends a local school, giving their family deep roots in the area.

As a Senior Software Management Engineer, Hitesh brings strong problem-solving skills and a practical approach. He is committed to being approachable, listening to residents, and working to improve local issues — from safer streets to cleaner neighbourhoods.

Cllr Wazma Qais, elected in 2019 and serving as a councillor for Silhill since then, is widely recognised for her consistent work on behalf of residents and her focus on local priorities. She has contributed to a range of improvements across Silhill, including challenging unsuitable planning applications, enhancing the Hermitage playground, supporting the repainting of the Streetsbrook Road bridge, and helping to secure the return of the Minor Injuries Unit to Solihull Hospital. She has also responded quickly to issues raised by residents, such as speeding concerns, parking problems, potholes, damaged pavements, and missed refuse collections.

As a strong voice for the community, she places residents’ concerns at the centre of her work. She is known for listening carefully, taking action promptly, and ensuring that local issues are addressed with attention to detail.

She has led local efforts to keep Silhill clean, founding the area’s first litter‑picking group and working to maintain streets free from litter and graffiti. Her commitment to community safety includes ongoing involvement in the campaign to retain Solihull Police Station.

Sardul lives in Monkspath. He was appointed as a Justice of the Peace in 1995. He is a TV presenter on the Sikh Channel.
Sardul has been involved in local politics and community affairs for over 30 years. In recognition of his services to the community, he was awarded the MBE in 1990, by Her Majesty the Queen.
He has previously served as: Labour Councillor, school governor, Chair of Police Consultative Committee, Chair of West Birmingham Health Authority, Vice-Chair of Sports Council. 
Sardul has the drive and experience to be an effective spokesperson on behalf of ALL of the people of Blythe. Time For A Change. http://www.solihulllabour.org.uk/blythe
